author: mm0x1
description: Reduce your mental load by sequencing your tasks in a drag-and-drop UI with compounding time estimates. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: mm0x1
manifest.description: Reduce your mental load by sequencing your tasks in a drag-and-drop UI with compounding time estimates.
manifest.name: Autism Task Planner

name: Autism Task Planner

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## v1.0.8

Fixes remaining issues flagged by ObsidianReviewBot:

- Removed explicit `constructor` methods from `TaskPlannerView` and `TaskPlannerSettingsTab` â€” this resolves a false-positive in the `obsidianmd/prefer-active-doc` rule caused by a prototype chain bug (the rule's `REPLACEMENTS` object accidentally matches `constructor` via `Object.prototype`). The plugin instance is now assigned after view construction in the `registerView` callback.
- Rephrased the default start time description to avoid the `HH:mm` notation that triggered the sentence-case rule in the bot's stricter configuration.
- Removed unused `App` and `WorkspaceLeaf` imports.
releases.name: v1.0.8

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## v1.0.7

Addresses all required issues flagged by ObsidianReviewBot:

- Sentence case applied to all UI text
- Removed `detachLeavesOfType` from `onunload` (prevented user-moved leaves from persisting their position)
- Fixed floating promises and promise-in-void-context issues
- Replaced manual `<h2>`/`<h3>` elements in settings with `Setting.setHeading()`
- Removed unnecessary type assertions
- Removed `async` from `onClose` (it had no `await` expression)
- Replaced `innerHTML` assignments with Obsidian's `setText()` helper
- Bumped `minAppVersion` to `1.7.2` to cover `revealLeaf` and `ColorComponent` API requirements
releases.name: v1.0.7

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## Bug fix

**Deleting tasks and then reordering created duplicate entries in the file.**

Deleting a line shifts every line below it up by one in the file, but the in-memory `lineIndex` values of remaining tasks were never updated. A subsequent reorder would read wrong file lines using those stale positions, writing scrambled content while leaving old lines untouched â€” producing duplicates.

`syncDeleteToFile` and `syncBulkDeleteToFile` now update `lineIndex` on all remaining tasks after each successful write.

## Design improvement

`modifyLines()` now accepts an optional `onSuccess` callback. Any sync operation that changes the number of lines in the file passes its index-update logic there. This ensures:
- Index updates only happen when the file write actually succeeds
- The requirement is visible at the callsite â€” a future method that splices lines and omits `onSuccess` is an obvious gap to spot in review
releases.name: v1.0.6 â€” Fix: task duplicates from delete + reorder

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## Bug fix

**Dragging tasks more than once produced scrambled file order.**

`task.lineIndex` was set at parse time and never updated when a reorder was synced to the file. On the second drag, `syncReorderToFile` read file lines using the original (now wrong) positions, writing scrambled content back.

Fix: `syncReorderToFile` now updates each task's `lineIndex` to its new position in the file immediately after writing, so all subsequent reorders and file operations use correct positions.
releases.name: v1.0.5 â€” Fix: reorder scrambles file after second drag

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Reorder after Reset All restored completed state** â€” reordering tasks after clicking Reset All would put them back to completed. Root cause: the reorder sync was using a stale in-memory snapshot of each line (`task.raw`) that still held the old `- [x]` content. Now reads the live file content instead.
- **Completing a `- [ ]` checkbox task didn't write to file** â€” the file sync guard was blocking all checkbox lines (`- [`), including unchecked ones. Now only skips already-completed `- [x]` lines.
- **Category edits were gated on the wrong sync toggle** â€” category changes now always write to file (explicit user action), regardless of toggle state.
- **Delete button was gated on sync completions toggle** â€” delete now always removes the line from file.
- **Reset All did N sequential file writes** â€” now a single bulk write.
- **`chore` and `chores` both appeared in dropdowns** â€” parser now normalises `-chore` â†’ `chores`.

## Code quality
- Extracted `modifyLines()` helper shared by all sync methods
- `capitalizeFirst` moved to the correct module
releases.name: v1.0.4 â€” Bug fixes and code cleanup

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## Bug fix

**Clear completed** now deletes non-recurring completed tasks from the markdown file in a single write operation. Previously it only removed them from the panel â€” reloading the note caused them to reappear.
releases.name: v1.0.3 â€” Fix: Clear completed removes tasks from file

releases.author: mm0x1
releases.description:
## What's new

**Recurring tasks** â€” add `~` to the end of any task to mark it as recurring:

\`\`\`
- feed the cat 2m -chores ~
- take vitamins 1m -health ~
\`\`\`

- Recurring tasks show a small **â†»** badge in the UI
- **Clear completed** skips recurring tasks â€” they stay in the list
- A new **Reset all** button appears (in the footer) whenever recurring tasks are completed â€” click it each morning to reset them all to incomplete
- Completion still syncs to file as `- [x]` normally; Reset all removes the checkbox
releases.name: v1.0.2 â€” Recurring tasks
